name: First Commerce Bank
description_short: First Commerce Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business banking services including checking, savings, money market accounts, CDs, and commercial lending.
description_long:
First Commerce Bank positions itself as a community-oriented financial institution backed by FDIC insurance and the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank operates through First Commerce Bancorp, Inc. and maintains an active digital presence at firstcommercebk.com.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Free Checking and Ultimate Rewards Checking), savings accounts (Relationship Savings and Premier Savings), money market accounts, and certificates of deposit with stated rates (7-month CD at 3.50% APY, 24-month CD at 3.25% APY). Personal customers can also access credit cards (Everyday Rewards+ and Travel Rewards+ cards), digital banking platforms, and appointment-based advisory services. The business banking division serves small businesses and nonprofits with dedicated checking accounts, money market products, IOLTA accounts, treasury management services (including ZSuites escrow solutions), ACH origination, sweep accounts, business credit cards, and commercial lending including mortgages and construction loans.

The bank distinguishes itself through community focus, tiered relationship banking with VIP perks for customers maintaining $250,000+ in combined balances, multilingual support (English, Hebrew, Spanish), nonprofit-specific offerings, and modern treasury management tools. They also offer ICS/CDARS services for FDIC coverage on larger deposits and Positive Pay fraud protection services.

As a regional community bank, First Commerce Bank serves customers seeking relationship banking with local presence and specialized services for nonprofits and small businesses. However, the website provides limited information about specific lending terms, approval requirements, account fees, or full APY rates across all products, requiring direct contact for detailed pricing. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal Checking Accounts (Free Checking, Ultimate Rewards Checking)
Personal Savings Accounts (Relationship Savings, Premier Savings)
Money Market Accounts (personal and business tiers)
Certificates of Deposit (7-month at 3.50% APY, 24-month at 3.25% APY)
Personal Credit Cards (Everyday Rewards+, Travel Rewards+)
Business Checking Accounts (Free, Premier, IOLTA, Non-Profit)
Treasury Management Services (ZSuites escrow and subaccounting)
ACH Origination Services (payroll, vendor payments, collections)
Merchant Services and Payment Processing (FCB/Banc Card)
Business Credit Cards (Business Cash Preferred, Smart Business Rewards)
Positive Pay fraud protection and ICS/CDARS deposit protection
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Tiered relationship banking with VIP perks for customers with $250,000+ combined balances
Specialized nonprofit banking division with dedicated Non-Profit Checking accounts
Modern treasury management tools including ZSuites for escrow and subaccounting
Multilingual website support in English, Hebrew, and Spanish
Comprehensive business lending including commercial mortgages and construction loans
ICS/CDARS services enabling multi-million-dollar FDIC coverage on deposits
cons: Website does not disclose specific account fees, monthly minimums, or overdraft charges
Limited transparency on CD rates—only two maturity options shown with rates that may not be competitive
No online account opening process visible; customers must request appointments for many services
Minimal information about personal loan products, eligibility criteria, or approval timelines
Website lacks details on digital banking features, mobile app capabilities, or ATM network access
best_for: Small business owners seeking relationship banking with local presence and commercial lending
Nonprofit organizations needing specialized checking and treasury management solutions
Community-oriented customers preferring local bank relationships over large national institutions
Businesses with $250,000+ in deposits seeking VIP relationship banking perks
diagnosis: First Commerce Bank is best for small business owners, nonprofits, and community-focused personal customers seeking relationship banking with local service and specialized commercial lending. The main caveat is that the website provides insufficient detail on fees, approval requirements, and competitive rates—prospective customers should contact the bank directly or visit local branches for specific terms before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
